Framework · free to use

2026 B2B AI vendor comparison matrix.

A side-by-side comparison of the 12 AI vendors mid-market B2B buyers most often evaluate. Built from observation, vendor documentation review, and direct buyer feedback through May 2026. Updated quarterly. Designed for buyers running real evaluations and analysts/journalists who need a citable structured comparison.

By Bill Colbert · Founder, Treetop Growth Strategy
Published May 2026 · More from the library
About this matrix

Methodology and citation

Purpose: A structured side-by-side comparison of the 12 AI vendors most often evaluated by B2B mid-market buyers in 2026. Designed for both buyers running real evaluations and analysts/journalists who need a citable comparison they can reference.

Scope: General-purpose LLM platforms, IDE coding tools, and the major enterprise AI suite vendors. Excludes pure infrastructure (Bedrock, Vertex) and very vertical-specific tools.

Permission to cite: Yes. Attribution: "Treetop Growth Strategy, 2026 B2B AI Vendor Comparison Matrix, May 2026 — treetopgrowthstrategy.com/b2b-ai-vendor-comparison-matrix-2026". Stable URL; quarterly refresh.

General-purpose LLM platforms

Core comparison

FeatureClaude (Anthropic)ChatGPT (OpenAI)Copilot (Microsoft)Gemini (Google)
Business tierTeam \$30/u/moTeam \$30/u/moM365 Copilot \$30/u/mo (req M365)Business \$20/u/mo (req Workspace)
Enterprise tierCustomCustomCustomCustom
Default model classSonnet 4.7GPT-5Mix (varies by surface)Gemini Advanced
Standard context window200K tokensVariable, largeVariableLarge
Max context window1M (some tiers)VariableVariableLarge
SSO/SCIMEnterpriseEnterpriseEnterpriseWorkspace tied
BAA availableEnterpriseEnterpriseEnterpriseEnterprise
No training on inputsTeam + EnterpriseTeam + EnterpriseBusiness + EnterpriseWorkspace tiers
Custom Projects/GPTsYes (Projects)Yes (Custom GPTs)Yes (Copilot Studio)Yes (Gems)
Image generationLimitedNativeLimitedNative
Web searchAvailableNative, polishedNativeNative
Audio/voiceLimitedNativeLimitedNative
Strongest forLong-form writing, nuance, calibrationBreadth, ecosystem, multimodalMicrosoft-heavy stacksGoogle Workspace customers
Coding-focused tools

Engineering team comparison

FeatureClaude CodeCursorGitHub CopilotWindsurf
SurfaceTerminal CLIIDE (VS Code fork)IDE inline + chatIDE (VS Code fork)
Pricing (business)Claude Team/Ent + APIPro \$20/u; Business \$40/uBusiness \$19/uPro \$15/u
Multi-file changesStrong (terminal-native)Strong (Composer mode)LimitedModerate
Large codebase reasoningStrongStrongLimitedModerate
Inline autocompleteN/A (terminal)StrongStrongStrong
Agentic workflowsStrongStrongLimitedModerate
Best forTerminal-comfortable engineers, refactoring, large codebasesIDE workflows + agentMicrosoft/GitHub-heavy teamsIDE-based agent workflows
Specialized AI vendor categories

What's commonly evaluated

Conversation intelligence + sales recording

  • Gong — market leader; recording + transcription + insights. Insights features mixed quality; recording + search still highest value.
  • Chorus — Zoominfo-owned; similar capability set to Gong; tighter Zoominfo integration.
  • Fathom — lighter-weight; growing among smaller teams.
  • Granola — newer; AI-native note-taking layered on recording; popular with execs.

Customer service AI

  • Gorgias — DTC/ecommerce focus; AI draft + agent assist features.
  • Intercom (Fin) — AI agent product; strong adoption; quality varies by use case.
  • Zendesk AI — built into the existing platform; reasonable but rarely lead reason to choose Zendesk.
  • Front (with AI features) — shared inbox with AI draft.

AI for marketing operations

  • HubSpot (with AI features) — native AI across the platform; usually best to use what's already in the stack.
  • Salesforce Einstein — improving; pricing premium often substantial.
  • Various 'AI marketing platforms' — most are thin wrappers on Claude/GPT; usually replaceable by Claude/ChatGPT Project work.
Decision guidance by buyer profile

Which combo fits which company

Buyer profileRecommended primary platformRecommended add-onsSkip
B2B SaaS \$5M-\$20M, technical teamClaude TeamClaude Code or Cursor for engineering; Gong for sales recordingMulti-vendor AI stacks; specialized AI suites
Professional services firm, mixed techClaude TeamStandard transcription (Fathom/Otter)Vertical-specific AI for verticals you do not serve
Microsoft-heavy enterprise (50+)Microsoft CopilotGitHub Copilot for engineeringAdding Claude unless specific gap
Google Workspace-heavy teamGemini BusinessAdd Claude for writing-heavy workflowsMicrosoft Copilot
DTC e-commerceClaude TeamGorgias AI for CS; Klaviyo for lifecycleSpecialized "AI for DTC" platforms
Regulated industry (healthcare, finserv)Claude Enterprise (BAA) or ChatGPT EnterpriseIndustry-specific tooling vetted for complianceFree or consumer tiers, ever
Pricing pitfalls

Common buyer mistakes

  • Buying multiple horizontal LLM platforms. One platform deeply adopted beats two platforms shallowly adopted, every time.
  • Paying for AI features in existing tools you do not use. Audit what's in your stack before adding.
  • Enterprise tier when Team would suffice. If you don't specifically need SSO, SCIM, or BAA, Team tier saves 40-60%.
  • API + UI double-pay. Building custom AI features on the API while also paying for UI seats — only do this if both surfaces are genuinely used.
  • Bundled pricing surprises. Microsoft Copilot requires M365; Gemini Business requires Workspace. Factor in the base license.
What's likely to change in the next 12 months

Vendor trajectory predictions

  • Capability convergence continues. Frontier model gap between Claude, GPT, Gemini narrows further. Differentiation moves to integration, workflow, and price.
  • Specialized 'AI feature' SaaS gets squeezed. Thin-wrapper vendors lose ground as horizontal LLM platforms absorb their capabilities.
  • Coding tool category consolidates. 2-3 winners emerge from the current 6-8 contenders. Cursor and Claude Code currently leading among engineers we observe.
  • Enterprise tier becomes more flexible. SSO, audit, BAA features expand to lower tiers; the rationale to upgrade narrows.
  • 'AI in your existing CRM/helpdesk/marketing tool' improves substantially. Re-evaluate dismissed AI features in tools you already pay for, in 6-12 months.
Methodology

How this matrix is maintained

  • Vendor pricing and feature data reviewed May 12-18, 2026.
  • Buyer evaluation feedback aggregated across ~60 mid-market engagements.
  • Capability assessments based on direct usage and structured comparison tests.
  • Subjective ratings ("strong," "moderate," "limited") reflect Treetop's evaluation; reasonable people may disagree.
  • Refresh cadence: quarterly. Next refresh: August 2026.
